news.title:
ICICI Prudential Q4 VNB rose 21.4% YoY to Rs 965 crore.
FY26 VNB margin improved to 24.7% from 22.8% YoY.
news.summary:
ICICI Prudential Q4 VNB rose 21.4% YoY to Rs 965 crore.
FY26 VNB margin improved to 24.7% from 22.8% YoY.

news.title:
Domestic MFs bought ₹34,800 crore in banking stocks, led by HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank, as FPIs exited ₹1.12 trillion in equities in March.
Bank Nifty fell 17% in March but rebounded 10% in April; MFs showed confidence in banking sector's long-term growth.
news.summary:
Domestic MFs bought ₹34,800 crore in banking stocks, led by HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank, as FPIs exited ₹1.12 trillion in equities in March.
Bank Nifty fell 17% in March but rebounded 10% in April; MFs showed confidence in banking sector's long-term growth.

news.title:
ICICI Bank schedules media call on April 18, 2026, at 4:00 PM IST.
Earnings call for analysts/investors to follow at 5:00 PM IST for Q4 FY26 results.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank schedules media call on April 18, 2026, at 4:00 PM IST.
Earnings call for analysts/investors to follow at 5:00 PM IST for Q4 FY26 results.

news.title:
ICICI Bank's board to meet on April 18, 2026, to discuss debt raising plans.
Plans include issuing debt securities via private placement and offshore deposits.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank's board to meet on April 18, 2026, to discuss debt raising plans.
Plans include issuing debt securities via private placement and offshore deposits.

news.title:
Macquarie favors HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank due to attractive valuations amid margin pressures in India's banking sector.
HDFC Life and SBI Life are also preferred. PSU banks face weak Q4 due to treasury losses and tight liquidity.
news.summary:
Macquarie favors HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank due to attractive valuations amid margin pressures in India's banking sector.
HDFC Life and SBI Life are also preferred. PSU banks face weak Q4 due to treasury losses and tight liquidity.

news.title:
Jefferies forecasts up to 42% rally in ICICI, Kotak, Shriram, citing stable growth, asset quality, and disciplined management.
ICICI target Rs.1730, Kotak Rs.530; vehicle, SME, insurance sectors show strong growth amid macro uncertainties.
news.summary:
Jefferies forecasts up to 42% rally in ICICI, Kotak, Shriram, citing stable growth, asset quality, and disciplined management.
ICICI target Rs.1730, Kotak Rs.530; vehicle, SME, insurance sectors show strong growth amid macro uncertainties.

news.title:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, CRISIL, and Swaraj Engines lead expected dividend announcements this week, with Swaraj Engines projecting Rs 109.2/share.
Investors should monitor record dates like Muthoot Finance's April 17 ex-dividend date and consider banking, engineering, and insurance sectors for opportunities.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, CRISIL, and Swaraj Engines lead expected dividend announcements this week, with Swaraj Engines projecting Rs 109.2/share.
Investors should monitor record dates like Muthoot Finance's April 17 ex-dividend date and consider banking, engineering, and insurance sectors for opportunities.

news.title:
Nifty braces for profit booking after US-Iran talks fail; crude oil prices surge above $100/barrel.
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Yes Bank results on Saturday; Nifty Bank surged 4,500 pts last week, key level 55,000.
news.summary:
Nifty braces for profit booking after US-Iran talks fail; crude oil prices surge above $100/barrel.
HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Yes Bank results on Saturday; Nifty Bank surged 4,500 pts last week, key level 55,000.

news.title:
8 of India's top 10 firms added ₹4.13 lakh crore market cap; HDFC, ICICI led gains.
Reliance stayed top-valued; Infosys, Reliance saw mcap erosion amid Sensex, Nifty surge.
news.summary:
8 of India's top 10 firms added ₹4.13 lakh crore market cap; HDFC, ICICI led gains.
Reliance stayed top-valued; Infosys, Reliance saw mcap erosion amid Sensex, Nifty surge.

news.title:
Key events this week include March inflation data, Wipro Q4 results, and special Parliament session on Women’s Reservation Bill.
Austrian Chancellor's visit and corporate earnings from HDFC, ICICI, and Yes Bank add to the week's economic momentum.
news.summary:
Key events this week include March inflation data, Wipro Q4 results, and special Parliament session on Women’s Reservation Bill.
Austrian Chancellor's visit and corporate earnings from HDFC, ICICI, and Yes Bank add to the week's economic momentum.

news.title:
Indian markets rose ~6% last week, driven by hopes of US-Iran ceasefire and supportive global cues.
Q4 FY26 earnings from Wipro, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank are key triggers; crude prices and US-Iran talks to influence outlook.
news.summary:
Indian markets rose ~6% last week, driven by hopes of US-Iran ceasefire and supportive global cues.
Q4 FY26 earnings from Wipro, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank are key triggers; crude prices and US-Iran talks to influence outlook.

news.title:
ICICI Bank executed an NSE block trade of ~468,392 shares worth Rs. 60.1 crore.
The trade was conducted at Rs. 1,283 per share.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank executed an NSE block trade of ~468,392 shares worth Rs. 60.1 crore.
The trade was conducted at Rs. 1,283 per share.

news.title:
SBI surpassed ICICI Bank in Q4 to become India's No.2 lender by market cap, despite a 0.3% decline to Rs 9,040.47B.
HDFC Bank retained top spot despite a 26.1% market cap drop, driven by chairman's resignation and sector-wide pressures.
news.summary:
SBI surpassed ICICI Bank in Q4 to become India's No.2 lender by market cap, despite a 0.3% decline to Rs 9,040.47B.
HDFC Bank retained top spot despite a 26.1% market cap drop, driven by chairman's resignation and sector-wide pressures.

news.title:
HDFC Bank ranks third among private lenders in large defaulter exposure, with dues of Rs 54,315 crore as of Feb 2026.
HDFC Bank's cautious lending keeps its exposure lower than ICICI Bank's Rs 2.78 lakh crore and Axis Bank's Rs 66,338 crore.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank ranks third among private lenders in large defaulter exposure, with dues of Rs 54,315 crore as of Feb 2026.
HDFC Bank's cautious lending keeps its exposure lower than ICICI Bank's Rs 2.78 lakh crore and Axis Bank's Rs 66,338 crore.

news.title:
JM Financial's Q4 playbook highlights sectors like IT, autos, telecom, and metals as key growth drivers. Preferred stocks include Infosys, Maruti Suzuki, ICICI Bank, Tata Steel, and Bharti Airtel.
Cautious on consumer, pharma, and utilities; stocks like HDFC Bank, Bajaj Finance, and LTIMindtree are marked 'avoid'. Report emphasizes sector-focused stock selection.
news.summary:
JM Financial's Q4 playbook highlights sectors like IT, autos, telecom, and metals as key growth drivers. Preferred stocks include Infosys, Maruti Suzuki, ICICI Bank, Tata Steel, and Bharti Airtel.
Cautious on consumer, pharma, and utilities; stocks like HDFC Bank, Bajaj Finance, and LTIMindtree are marked 'avoid'. Report emphasizes sector-focused stock selection.

news.title:
Nomura sees limited upside for PSU banks, prefers private banks like Axis Bank, ICICI Bank, and Kotak Mahindra Bank due to favorable valuations.
PSU banks' rally driven by cyclical factors; private banks offer stronger earnings visibility, trading below historical valuation levels.
news.summary:
Nomura sees limited upside for PSU banks, prefers private banks like Axis Bank, ICICI Bank, and Kotak Mahindra Bank due to favorable valuations.
PSU banks' rally driven by cyclical factors; private banks offer stronger earnings visibility, trading below historical valuation levels.

news.title:
BofA sees upside in Indian bank stocks due to attractive valuations, with HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank trading below historical averages.
IT sector faces short-term AI disruption, despite rupee-driven rally; BofA rates banks 'Overweight' and IT 'Underweight'.
news.summary:
BofA sees upside in Indian bank stocks due to attractive valuations, with HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank trading below historical averages.
IT sector faces short-term AI disruption, despite rupee-driven rally; BofA rates banks 'Overweight' and IT 'Underweight'.

news.title:
Nifty 50 and Sensex rebounded as US-Iran ceasefire led Nifty to rise 3.5% to 23,938 and Sensex 3.7% to 77,392.
Large-cap stocks like DLF, M&M, HDFC Life, LIC, Adani Ports, ICICI Bank show potential upside up to 63% amid valuation gaps.
news.summary:
Nifty 50 and Sensex rebounded as US-Iran ceasefire led Nifty to rise 3.5% to 23,938 and Sensex 3.7% to 77,392.
Large-cap stocks like DLF, M&M, HDFC Life, LIC, Adani Ports, ICICI Bank show potential upside up to 63% amid valuation gaps.

news.title:
Bank Nifty poised for a 3-4% rally amid ceasefire and RBI policy decision, likely outperforming Nifty50.
Key stocks like HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, SBI, and Axis Bank may benefit; RBI expected to hold rates at 5.25%.
news.summary:
Bank Nifty poised for a 3-4% rally amid ceasefire and RBI policy decision, likely outperforming Nifty50.
Key stocks like HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, SBI, and Axis Bank may benefit; RBI expected to hold rates at 5.25%.

news.title:
BofA Securities remains bullish on ICICI Bank and HDFC Bank despite cutting target prices.
SBI has been upgraded to a 'Buy' rating by BofA Securities.
news.summary:
BofA Securities remains bullish on ICICI Bank and HDFC Bank despite cutting target prices.
SBI has been upgraded to a 'Buy' rating by BofA Securities.
